reply_text:
As a manga reader, I disagree with the sentiment that this should've ended at season 2.
Even without the announcement of ss3, I've already sensed a secret story happening from miles away.
4 questions:

- What's actually The Borderlands? The way it operates and keep pulling ppl in/out is still a real mystery. You don't need to explain it fully, but at least ppl need to know the nature of it. That could be a way more satisfying detail when included in the actual ending. 
- (manga spoiler) Why is there a Joker? I mean a person represents the Joker card. What's his role? Is there a real master of all these things that's actually untouchable by the system?
- Is there any way to control the game, instead of being controlled by it? If the winner of the face cards can establish their own games, that means there could be some kind of special condition for it to happen. 
- No way the source material developed 2 of the best potential villains of the series in ss2, then sent them off by locking them inside the Borderland. We didn't even have any backstory of them. They're the trump card for anything beyond the happy go lucky ending of ss2.

These are just my opinion. Feel free to comment further on it.
